Node.js Tips and Tricks

2026-03-03 by Robin
nodejs javascript tutorial

Node.js Tips and Tricks

Here are some useful tips for working with Node.js effectively.

1. Use Path Module Correctly

Always use path.join() instead of string concatenation:

const path = require(class="hl-string">'path');

class="hl-comment">// Good
const filePath = path.join(__dirname, class="hl-string">'data', class="hl-string">'config.json');

class="hl-comment">// Bad
const badPath = __dirname + class="hl-string">'/data/config.json';

2. Read Files Efficiently

For smaller files, readFileSync is fine. For larger files, use streams:

const fs = require(class="hl-string">'fs');

class="hl-comment">// Stream approach for large files
const stream = fs.createReadStream(class="hl-string">'large-file.txt', {
  encoding: class="hl-string">'utf-class="hl-number">8',
  highWaterMark: class="hl-number">1024
});

stream.on(class="hl-string">'data', (chunk) => {
  class="hl-comment">// process chunk
});

3. Error Handling

Always handle errors properly in async code:

async function loadData() {
  try {
    const data = await fs.promises.readFile(class="hl-string">'data.json', class="hl-string">'utf-class="hl-number">8');
    return JSON.parse(data);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error(class="hl-string">'Failed to load data:', err.message);
    return null;
  }
}
These patterns will help you write more robust Node.js applications.
